title: sloanea monosperma var. pulverulenta
intro: Sloanea monosperma var. pulverulenta, also known as the white sloanea, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Eribetiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating cough, and treating respiratory infections. The leaves and flowers of Sloanea monosperma var. pulverulenta are used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, to harness its medicinal properties.
